behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: collectors -> aggregation payload -> summary generator input (MOCK-MISS=yes for cross-module wiring). No real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce this specific failure; source clients can be stubbed (REAL-INFRA no). Correctness does not depend on model output content, only on what is passed into the step (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because behavior text is about input composition, not real endpoint behavior; Agentic is disqualified because it does not assert summary quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real data-collection/assembly modules with stub adapters for metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log. Run one report build and assert the exact payload handed to summary generation contains fields from all three sources, including source identifiers/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience behavior across modules (fetcher error -> section renderer/report assembler), so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS=yes). A simulated source failure from an in-process stub is sufficient to trigger the real fallback path (REAL-INFRA no). No dependency on LLM judgment quality is required for pass/fail; assertion is presence of fallback note and continued report generation (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher levels: System disqualified because behavior does not require real network outage mechanics; Agentic disqualified because it does not evaluate model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source adapters returning data and one adapter raising an availability error. Assert process completes, output contains the unavailable-note in the correct section, and other sections are still populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model-output quality (LLM-DEP=yes): whether generated narrative faithfully reflects key values is a model judgment problem that stubs would mask (MOCK-HIDE=yes). BOUNDED TWO-PASS: Pass 1 intent tag = model-output; Pass 2 label map => Agentic. Rejected Workflow: behavior describes one summary-generation decision, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summary-generation model/API with representative weekly input fixtures and evaluation checks for factual fidelity (key metrics/issues/deployments present, no contradictions). Run repeated trials and fail if fidelity drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external infrastructure: end-to-end delivery to an actual inbox over real email transport/network (REAL-INFRA=yes). BOUNDED TWO-PASS: Pass 1 intent tag = real-infra; Pass 2 label map => System. Agentic is not required as minimum because this behavior's assertion is delivery to inbox, not model-output quality. Rejected Workflow: no requirement for multiple agentic decisions; rejected Agentic due disqualifying text focused on transport outcome ('reaches ... inbox').
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled script (or equivalent entrypoint) against reachable test endpoints and a real test mailbox. Verify the email is received by configured recipients with expected subject/body and attachments/sections present.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is orchestration/state handoff across runs: send failure -> local persistence -> next-run retry. This can be reproduced with module wiring, a failing mail adapter, persisted local file, and second invocation (MOCK-MISS=yes; REAL-INFRA not strictly required). No model-output-quality dependence (LLM-DEP no) and no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior text does not require proving real SMTP/network semantics, only fallback and retry logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run two sequential invocations with shared local state path. First run uses mail adapter that raises unreachable error; assert report file is saved. Second run uses successful adapter; assert saved report is retried/sent and local fallback file/state is cleared or marked delivered.
